title: multiplication (signed * unsigned)
body:
I am trying to implement RISC-V (RV32IM) 32-bit signed * unsigned multiplication with simple non optimized code:
```SystemVerilog
mul [64-1:0] = $signed(a[32-1:0]) * $unsigned(b[32-1:0]);
```
I expected it to work, but the RISC-V architecture tests fail on the next example: 
https://github.com/jeras/rp32/tree/master/issues/mul

```
a = -32'h20000001;
b = -32'h20000001;
```
If I perform the calculation with literals I get the right result:
```
a = 0xdfffffff =  -536870913, b = 0xdfffffff = 3758096383
r64ws = 0xe3ffffff40000001 = -2017612636283207679
r64ds = 0xe3ffffff40000001 = -2017612636283207679
```
But if I use signals `$signed(a) * $unsigned(b)` I get a wrong result:
```
signed/unsigned multiplication:
r64ws = 0xc3fffffe40000001 = -4323455649791868927
r64ds = 0xc3fffffe40000001 = -4323455649791868927
```

I tried to simulate in Rivera-PRO on [edaplayground](https://www.edaplayground.com/x/dyaU), but I get the same result as with Verilator.
I also tried with smaller vectors, in case the issue is related to 32-bit integer types, but I get the same behavior.

Is there something I am doing wrong, is this a bug in both tools, ...

The SystemVerilog LRM does not seem to provide any relevant details here, I only found section 11.4.3.1 to be somehow relevant.

Verilator here reports a width warning, again I am not sure what should be the width of a multiplication result according to the LRM.
